Prior to amendment, second sentence read as follows: “The items of income referred to in subsection (a) from which tax shall be deducted and withheld at the rate of 14 percent are amounts which are received by a nonresident alien individual who is temporarily present in the United States as a nonimmigrant under subparagraph (F) or (J) of section 101(a)(15) of the Immigration and Nationality Act and which are incident to a qualified scholarship to which section 117(a) applies, but only to the extent such amounts are includible in gross income.”Pub. L. 99–514, § 2100 Stat. 2095Sept. 3, 1982Oct. 22, 1986“Not later than 2 years after the date of the enactment of this Act [], the Secretary of the Treasury or his delegate shall prescribe regulations establishing certification procedures, refund procedures, or other procedures which ensure that any benefit of any treaty relating to withholding of tax under sections 1441 and 1442 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 [formerly I.R.C. 1954] is available only to persons entitled to such benefit.”Sept. 3, 1982

children.heading: General rule
children.label: a General rule
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in subsection (c), all persons, in whatever capacity acting (including lessees or mortgagors of real or personal property, fiduciaries, employers, and all officers and employees of the United States) having the control, receipt, custody, disposal, or payment of any of the items of income specified in subsection (b) (to the extent that any of such items constitutes gross income from sources within the United States), of any nonresident alien individual or of any foreign partnership shall (except as otherwise provided in regulations prescribed by the Secretary under section 874) deduct and withhold from such items a tax equal to 30 percent thereof, except that in the case of any item of income specified in the second sentence of subsection (b), the tax shall be equal to 14 percent of such item.
incident to a qualified scholarship to which section 117(a) applies, but only to the extent includible in gross income; or

children.label: A
children.text: an organization described in section 501(c)(3) which is exempt from tax under section 501(a),

children.label: B
children.text: a foreign government,

children.label: C
children.text: an international organization, or a binational or multinational educational and cultural foundation or commission created or continued pursuant to the Mutual Educational and Cultural Exchange Act of 1961, or

children.label: D
children.text: the United States, or an instrumentality or agency thereof, or a State, or a possession of the United States, or any political subdivision thereof, or the District of Columbia,

children.heading: Income connected with United States business
children.label: 1 Income connected with United States business
children.text: No deduction or withholding under subsection (a) shall be required in the case of any item of income (other than compensation for personal services) which is effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business within the United States and which is included in the gross income of the recipient under section 871(b)(2) for the taxable year.

children.heading: Owner unknown
children.label: 2 Owner unknown
children.text: The Secretary may authorize the tax under subsection (a) to be deducted and withheld from the interest upon any securities the owners of which are not known to the withholding agent.

children.heading: Bonds with extended maturity dates
children.label: 3 Bonds with extended maturity dates
children.text: The deduction and withholding in the case of interest on bonds, mortgages, or deeds of trust or other similar obligations of a corporation, within subsections (a), (b), and (c) of section 1451 (as in effect before its repeal by the Tax Reform Act of 1984) were it not for the fact that the maturity date of such obligations has been extended on or after , and the liability assumed by the debtor exceeds 27½ percent of the interest, shall not exceed the rate of 27½ percent per annum.January 1, 1934

children.heading: Compensation of certain aliens
children.label: 4 Compensation of certain aliens
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, compensation for personal services may be exempted from deduction and withholding under subsection (a).

children.heading: Special items
children.label: 5 Special items
children.text: In the case of gains described in section 631(b) or (c), and gains subject to tax under section 871(a)(1)(D), the amount required to be deducted and withheld shall, if the amount of such gain is not known to the withholding agent, be such amount, not exceeding 30 percent of the amount payable, as may be necessary to assure that the tax deducted and withheld shall not be less than 30 percent of such gain.

children.heading: Per diem of certain aliens
children.label: 6 Per diem of certain aliens
children.text: No deduction or withholding under subsection (a) shall be required in the case of amounts of per diem for subsistence paid by the United States Government (directly or by contract) to any nonresident alien individual who is engaged in any program of training in the United States under the Mutual Security Act of 1954, as amended.

children.heading: Certain annuities received under qualified plans
children.label: 7 Certain annuities received under qualified plans
children.text: No deduction or withholding under subsection (a) shall be required in the case of any amount received as an annuity if such amount is, under section 871(f), exempt from the tax imposed by section 871(a).

children.heading: Original issue discount
children.label: 8 Original issue discount
children.text: The Secretary may prescribe such regulations as may be necessary for the deduction and withholding of the tax on original issue discount subject to tax under section 871(a)(1)(C) including rules for the deduction and withholding of the tax on original issue discount from payments of interest.

children.heading: Interest income from certain portfolio debt investments
children.label: 9 Interest income from certain portfolio debt investments
children.text: In the case of portfolio interest (within the meaning of section 871(h)), no tax shall be required to be deducted and withheld from such interest unless the person required to deduct and withhold tax from such interest knows, or has reason to know, that such interest is not portfolio interest by reason of section 871(h)(3) or (4).

children.heading: Exception for certain interest and dividends
children.label: 10 Exception for certain interest and dividends
children.text: No tax shall be required to be deducted and withheld under subsection (a) from any amount described in section 871(i)(2).

children.heading: Certain gambling winnings
children.label: 11 Certain gambling winnings
children.text: No tax shall be required to be deducted and withheld under subsection (a) from any amount exempt from the tax imposed by section 871(a)(1)(A) by reason of section 871(j).

children.heading: Certain dividends received from regulated investment companies
children.label: 12 Certain dividends received from regulated investment companies

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No tax shall be required to be deducted and withheld under subsection (a) from any amount exempt from the tax imposed by section 871(a)(1)(A) by reason of section 871(k).

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: B Special rule
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), clause (i) of section 871(k)(1)(B) shall not apply to any dividend unless the regulated investment company knows that such dividend is a dividend referred to in such clause. A similar rule shall apply with respect to the exception contained in section 871(k)(2)(B).

children.heading: Exemption of certain foreign partnerships
children.label: d Exemption of certain foreign partnerships
children.text: Subject to such terms and conditions as may be provided by regulations prescribed by the Secretary, subsection (a) shall not apply in the case of a foreign partnership engaged in trade or business within the United States if the Secretary determines that the requirements of subsection (a) impose an undue administrative burden and that the collection of the tax imposed by section 871(a) on the members of such partnership who are nonresident alien individuals will not be jeopardized by the exemption.

children.heading: Alien resident of Puerto Rico
children.label: e Alien resident of Puerto Rico
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “nonresident alien individual” includes an alien resident of Puerto Rico.

children.heading: Continental shelf areas
children.label: f Continental shelf areas
children.text: For sources of income derived from, or for services performed with respect to, the exploration or exploitation of natural resources on submarine areas adjacent to the territorial waters of the United States, see section 638.

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: g Cross reference
children.text: For provision treating 85 percent of social security benefits as subject to withholding under this section, see section 871(a)(3).
